feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Apps
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Apps blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - creating a CRUD application
entry.content:
This blog post is the eighth in a series of posts that cover the connectivity options available for SAP Build Apps to interface with SAP systems. The series will cover connecting SAP Build Apps with the following:

  1. CAP-based services
  2. S/4HANA systems
  3. ABAP systems

The format of this post is similar to a tutorial. However, I'll provide more comprehensive details, tips and the opportunity for you to provide feedback. Based on the feedback we will be able to adjust and enhance this post and future parts of the series, and might even be able to improve the products involved as well. 

Introduction 

Before we get into the details of setting up connections between SAP Build Apps and SAP Systems, I intend to introduce a couple of SAP technology components in each post that are relevant for the use cases covered in the series.

CRUD operations

The acronym CRUD stands for Create, Read, Update, Delete. CRUD operations refer to the four basic functions of persistent storage. In the context of OData (Open Data Protocol), these operations are used to interact with data services.

Take note that when interacting with a data service, you are not only storing or retrieving data. The service can also implement (business) logic that is executed when receiving such requests. E.g. for data validation, or triggering another process. Besides that, when looking at access control discussed in part 2 of this blog series, we can configure access to these operations individually for each user.

Use case H : creating a CRUD application in SAP Build Apps

The application we have built so far in this series only shows lists of information. There is no way for the user to create new records, update existing records, or delete records. In this blog post we will change the application we've built in SAP Build Apps into a so-called CRUD application, allowing users to make change to the data stored in the database.

Assuming we are starting a new development session, we open the SAP Build lobby.

As visible in the list of SAP Build projects, we now have 2 versions of the UI application created in SAP Build Apps. Let's open this by selecting the link.

SAP Build Apps opens, and notifies us that there are updates in the marketplace. This is due to a recent release of updates that include components released in the marketplace.

Clicking on the notification brings us to marketplace updates.

Select INSTALL UPDATES to retrieve the latest updates for the flow functions covering create, update and delete a record and SAVE your updated project. These updates are actually quite relevant for this use case!

We can test the current app in Preview to see whether these changes had any impact on our app. When selecting Preview, you will be able to pick the version. We'll pick the latest.

Just as to showcase the starting point of our development, here is a screenshot of the current web preview. It is a very basic list without pagination.

Notice that I've deployed some more initial sample data for the service created in SAP Build Code. This will help while testing the page, for which will also implement the data pagination.

Update the data entity used in our project

Before we get started, we need to update our project to the latest changes in the data service. When we started developing the application, the data entity Products only contained the properties ID, name and price. In the previous blog post we have added a relationship to Supplier, which is stored in a S/4HANA Cloud system.

To update the project, simply navigate to the DATA tab in SAP Build Apps and select the OData integration for Products. The Data entities overview will show the additional field a_Supplier_Supplier, which we've added. However, this simply reflects the current information retrieved from the data service. This might change over time, as new versions of the service are released. Your project in SAP Build Apps does not automatically update to reflect these changes.

To update your project, select DISABLE DATA ENTITY, followed by ENABLE DATA ENTITY for the data entity Products, to ensure the newly added property Supplier is included in your project.

 

Creating a new page to replace the existing one

Instead of changing the existing starting page of the app, we will start by creating a new one. This allows you to refer to a fully functioning page, while we create a replacement. In case you misconfigured something, you can easily check the old page.

Navigate to the pages overview and select ADD NEW PAGE. Provide a name for the page. In this case, I am assigning it the name "Products List", which is going to replace the page "Blog Demo App".

Once the UI canvas editor opens, switch to the VARIABLES and navigate to the DATA VARIABLES tab. Select ADD DATA VARIABLE and pick the Products entity. Rename the data variable to ProductsList. Open the logic editor and connect the Page focused event to the Get record collection. Whenever a user navigates to this page, we will refresh the record collection. This ensures we see the latest updates reflected in the list.


We also add Page Variables to handle the pagination. This will be similar to what we created in blog episode 4, but we'll use a different UI control here and a slightly different configuration.


Adding the list control and page navigation

Switching back to UI Canvas View, we will add list item, navigation buttons and a text field to indicate the page number.  

To prevent the user from navigating to invalid pages, we'll make use of the Disabled property on the buttons. For the left button, we control the property with the following formula.

 

IF(pageVars.CurrentPage>1, false, true)

 

For the right button, we'll disable it to prevent the user from navigating beyond the last page. For this, configure the Disabled property and bind it to the formula:

 

IF(pageVars.CurrentPage<pageVars.TotalPageCount, false, true)

 

For the displaying the current and total amount of pages, we simply bind the Content property to the formula

 

"Page: "+pageVars.CurrentPage + " of "+pageVars.TotalPageCount

 


We'll need to add some logic to both buttons to increase or decrease the current page number respectively. Select the button and open the logic canvas.


For the left button, we set the page variable CurrentPage as follows:

 

pageVars.CurrentPage-1

 

Since we disable the button once CurrentPage is 1, we can keep this formula simple.

For the right button, we set the page variable CurrentPage as follows:

 

pageVars.CurrentPage+1

 


After updating the CurrentPage variable, we configure "Get record collection" with pagination as below.

And after this, we will set the data variable ProductsList to the Output value of the previous node.

We apply the above for the both buttons.


Getting the total amount of records

The page variable TotalPageCount is based on the total amount of records in the database divided by the amount of items we display per page. In this example we will use a fixed number of 10 items per page. You could implement more sophisticated paging that fills the visible screen or window, but we'll keep it simple here.


Select the page canvas and open the logic editor. We will get the record collection of Products, followed by setting the page variable TotalPageCount as follows:

 

INTEGER((outputs["Get record collection"].totalCount -1) / 10) + 1

 

The formula uses the output of "Get record collection" to find out the total amount of records. To prevent loading all data, adding unnecessary overhead and even potential crashes, we need to customise this data request. We configure the property Paging of Get record collection. For the binding, select Object with properties, configured as shown below.

Since we don't need to load any data to retrieve the total record count, we will set the page size and page number to zero. What's relevant for now, is to Include total count.

Switch back to the VARIABLES view and update the logic flow of ProductsList. For Get record collection, we configure Paging.

And we store the result of Get record collection in the ProductsList variable.

 

Adding the new page into the application's navigation flow

Now that we are done with the list and the pagination, we add the page into the application's navigation flow. Go to the NAVIGATION tab and select Add item. Select the page we have developed. Give it a meaningful name and assign it a suitable icon.

 

Previewing and testing the updated app

Navigate to LAUNCH and select Open preview portal. Select Open web preview and use the latest version to Open preview. If all is configured correctly, we should see a list of 10 products and are able to use the buttons to navigate the pages.

 

Adding Create, Update and Delete

In the previous section, we have covered the Read operations for this CRUD app. It is now time to add the functionality for Create, Update and Delete.

Navigate to the pages overview and select ADD NEW PAGE. Provide a name for the page. In this case, I am assigning it the name "Create new product".

Once the page is opened in UI Canvas editor, switch to the variables and create a new data variable for storing the new record. Add a new data variable based on Products. As for the data variable type, select New data record. For the name of the data variable, I am using "newProduct".

On the UI Canvas, drag and drop a Button and a few Input fields. Bind the value of the input fields, to the newProduct record items. 

For the Create button, we need to add some more logic.

On receiving the component tap event, we create a new data record in Products. Upon success, we briefly show a toast message and navigate back to the previous page. Upon failure, we will show an alert and stay on the current page.

As for the configuration of Create record, we use the following custom object:

For ID, we will generate UUID using a formula. The properties name and price are assigned the corresponding values in the newProduct record.

Adding a detail page for Read, Update and Delete

Following the master-detail UI pattern, we will now add a new page that shows the details of a selected record.

Navigate to the pages overview and select ADD NEW PAGE. Provide a name for the page. In this case, I am assigning it the name "Product Details".

Once the page is opened in UI Canvas editor, switch to the variables and create a new data variable for the storing the selected record.

We add a page parameter called productID of type UUID.

Add a new data variable based on Products. As for the data variable type, select New data record. For the name of the data variable, I am using "selectedProduct", and the ID will be the previously created productID.

Going back to the UI canvas, we drag and drop a few buttons and input fields.

For the Delete button, we add some more logic. When the Delete button is pressed, we ask for confirmation using a dialog. If confirmed, we delete the record with ID productID from Products. When successful, we navigate back to the previous page. If deletion fails, we show an alert to inform the user.

For the Update button, we add a similar logic flow. When the Update button is pressed, we update the record with ID productID of Products. When successful, we navigate back to the previous page. If the update fails, we show an alert to inform the user.

Now that we are done with the Product Details page, we will configure the page navigation for accessing the new pages we have added to the app.

Adding page navigation

We go back to the newly created Products List page, and drag and drop a button onto the page. I've also removed the Title and the Text on top of the page to make room for a button.

Upon receiving the Component tap event, we open the 'Create new product' page, and provide the selected item as parameter.

 

After testing the app in preview, and are happy with the new page, we can remove the old Products List and replace it with the new one. We also rename the new page and move it to the top of the navigation list.


Resolving an interoperability issue between SAP Build Apps and CAP version 8

At the time of writing this blog post, we have identified an issue with OData create and update operations initiated from SAP Build Apps. The data service based on CAP CDS version 8 does no longer support the property odata.type and reports error 400 if you do try to use it. We are working to resolve this issue, while making sure all other integrations keep working as expected.

If you run into this issue, you can opt for using the old protocol adapter in your data service created in SAP Build Coce by configuring the following:

 

cds.features.odata_new_adapter = false

 

As a temporary workaround, you can add the following in your project's package.json file:

Once you redeploy your data service, the create and update operations will work again.
More information on the use of protocol adaptors in CAP can be found in the CAP documentation. 

Not the most efficient implementation

Is this the most perfect and efficient configuration of a CRUD app in SAP Build Apps? Not likely. That was not the goal of this blog post. I have tried to keep things simple. There is surely room for improvement, such as:

  • Trigger less OData service requests
  • Make use of entity data already received, instead of sending another query
  • Faster trigger of queries when navigating pages

Stay tuned for improvements in this area, while we are adding more features to SAP Build Apps.

The end-result on Mobile (preview app)

Below is a screen recording of the app running on my iPhone inside the SAP Build Apps app. I am showing a list of products (Read), Adding a new product (Create), checking whether the pagination counts the correct number of pages, update to price of a selected product (Update) and finally remove a selected product (Delete).

Summary – use case G

In this use case we have replaced a simple list view page with a CRUD page. Instead of modifying the existing page, we've introduced a new one, that eventually replaced the original page.

As a first step, we've added pagination. This avoids loading lot's of data coming from an S/4HANA Cloud system. 

Next, we've added a page for adding new products and add a button to navigate to this page.

Follow the master-detail UI pattern, when a user selects a product from the list, a new page will open that shows all the details of the product. In our example we have Supplier as additional information. But there is surely room for more. The detail page allows users to update the record. We've also added a button to delete the record.

What's next

In the next blog post, we will continue on the connectivity topic and move our focus to connecting to ABAP systems.

entry.title: Duplicating (and Sharing) Pages in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
New feature alert: You can now duplicate pages in SAP Build Apps.

Go to the link for the current page and click it.

Now you will get the pages UI, and here you can create or delete pages. You can now duplicate a page.

I'm not sure how all the styles and layouts copy, because I set a specific layout for a custom component on a page, and then when I copied the page the layout was slightly different. But it could have been how I saved the layout changes. I will keep experimenting.

 

Sharing Pages

Since it's kind of related, I thought I'd share how you can share pages among your colleagues.

Put everything on your page inside a container – I haven't found a super easy way to do this, since I have to drag each existing component into the new container one at a time, and remember to do it from bottom to top. 👉 If you know of an easier way, let me know.

Select the container, and copy it with Control-C (Windows) or Command-C (Mac). You now have on the clipboard a text representation of the page that you can share with others. Here's a part of it.

Then go to the UI canvas where you want to copy it, and paste it with Control-V (Windows) or Command-V (Mac).

This used to be the way for individuals within a project to copy a page. You can still use this to copy specific containers or to share components.

entry.title: Flow Function of the Month: Dismiss Keyboard 🏆
entry.content:
You know that feeling when you have a coding problem and then you find that there exists an object or method or property that gives you the exact thing you need?

I was building an ice breaker app for our next CodeJam (by the way, you can request an SAP Build or any CodeJam anywhere in the world on our CodeJam page). I created a form to let people answer some fun questions about themselves, and then display everyone's answer.

Now when I clicked the "Tell Everybody" button, the data was submitted. But if the user did not explicitly dismiss the keyboard, it remained, and this to me was really annoying, especially since there seemed no button in the keyboard to dismiss itself and it scrolled the page so I couldn't see most of it.

To my good fortune, there is a somewhat lesser known "Dismiss Keyboard" flow function, that does exactly what I needed. You'll have to import from the Marketplace.

And I could put it right after I handle the (successful) submit button click.

Now, after the user submits the form, the keyboard goes away and I can see the entire page again. 

😺

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What's New in Q3 2024
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP is always evolving to better serve our customers. With the latest update in Q3 2024, in addition to quarterly content maintenance and a new product page we've made significant improvements to the user experience:

  1. New Side Menu for improved page navigation
  2. New UI Elements for additional context
  3. New Search Feature 

Read more about the details of these enhancements in this blog post.

This section details the new UX features added to the release navigator for SAP BTP.

New Side Menu for Easier Navigation

We've implemented a new side menu on every page of the release navigator to provide a more intuitive user experience. This menu allows easy navigation to the four new main pages of the release navigator:

  1. Product Specific
  2. General
  3. Get Assistance
  4. User Feedback

Fig 1. The side menu on all new pages

As a result, some content previously found on the Home page has been rearranged. Let’s walk through what you can find on each of the new pages.

Product Specific Page

This page serves as the starting point for the release navigator and corresponds to the former Home page. It contains the core of the release navigator: curated release information for specific products and services of SAP BTP as well as information on release schedules.

Fig 2. The Product Specific page, formerly known as the Home page

At the top of the Product Specific Page you can find a message tile with helpful information and a Release Calendar. The calendar includes release dates for SAP BTP as a whole, links to product-specific release schedules and dates for upcoming release navigator updates.

Below you can find the section Products. The cards in this section are sorted alphabetically and each one takes you to a dedicated page with in-depth information and curated resources on the 27 SAP BTP products and services covered.

Product Page Navigation

Fig 3. Example of a dedicated product page

Once you are done exploring the resources on a product page and want to move on to the next product or service, you can either click on Product Specific on the side-menu or on the back arrow on the left of the page title to get back to the Product Specific page and navigate to other pages.

General Page

On this page you can find General Resources that are helpful across all SAP BTP products and services. If you can't find a certain product or service on the Product Specific page, we recommend you check out the resources on this page (and also feel free to let us know in our survey).

Besides the General resources, there are two more sections on this page:

  • Additional Resources contains non-release specific but nevertheless important resources one should be aware of.
  • Release Navigators by SAP Enterprise Support provide direct links to all currently available release navigators for other solution areas.

Fig 4. The General page

Get Assistance Page

This page contains resources to help you use the release navigator and to improve your experience. You can access it from all pages in the release navigator by either clicking on Get Assistance in the side-menu or by clicking on the red bar at the very top of the page.

At the top of the page, you can find the following elements:

  • Release Navigator Enablement:
    • SAP Community Blog Posts: These blog posts go into detail about new features that are being added to the release navigator and provide other news.
    • SAP Partner Masterclass: This recorded digital session is only available to SAP partners.
  • Translation Feature: Allows you to translate the release navigator (not the pages linked to) into 40 languages. The translation process takes a few moments and may impact layout and design of the navigator pages. Click on this card to sign in to the authenticated version with Universal ID or an S-user account to use this feature.
  • User Feedback: Click here to provide feedback in a quick 3 question survey.

Below you can find the Navigation Tips, detailed instructions on how to use the release navigator, similar to the blog post you are reading now.

Fig 5. The Get Assistance page

User Feedback Page

We've embedded a feedback survey on this page with three short questions to gather feedback and suggestions for improvement.

  • Your relationship to SAP.
  • Whether you found the release navigator helpful.
  • An open text field that allows you to let us know anything you liked, what you would like to see added, or what you think could be improved.

We strive to always improve the release navigator, so we really appreciate any feedback you submit.

Fig 6. The User Feedback page

Helpful UI Elements

We added two new types of UI elements that you will find throughout the pages of the navigator.

Lightbulb icon

Many pages come with additional information to guide you through the cards available on that page and provide more context on when to use which resource.

If you see a lightbulb icon next to a header, you can hover over it with your cursor to open a text bubble with additional information about that page.

The bubble can be moved around by clicking and holding your cursor and can be closed by clicking the  on the top right.Fig 7. Demo of the lightbulb icon text bubble

On product pages, the information displayed in this bubble corresponds to the information formerly found on Release Guidance tiles.

About this Card

Some resource cards come with additional tips and contextual information.

If you see three dots on the bottom right of a card, you can open a tip with additional information about that particular card. To open the link of that card, you need to close this tip by clicking the three dots button again.

Fig 8. Demo on the About this Card tips

Due to the high demand for dedicated pages for products and services, the number of pages to navigate has grown significantly since launch of the release navigator one year ago and we aim to increase the number of pages even further in future updates. More pages mean more content but also more cards to browse, which can increase the time it takes to find what you’re looking for.

Our goal is for you to find what you need as quickly as possible. That’s why we implemented a new Search Feature, which allows you to search for keywords and find all pages associated with this topic, product or service name. Click on the  Search icon at the shell bar and a pop-up will open. Once you enter key words, you will see which pages are associated to this term and you can jump directly to them. The search feature is optimized for product and service names.

Fig 9. Short demo of the Search function.

We collaborated closely with SAP BTP experts to add a new page for SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery. This page contains links to the release schedule, SAP Roadmap Explorer, What’s New Viewer in the SAP Help Portal and more. Check it out.

Fig 10. The new page for SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery.

Currently, the release navigator for SAP BTP covers content for 27 products and services on dedicated pages. For all products and services not featured with their own page you can find helpful resources on the new General page.

Expect more new pages dedicated to specific products and services in future updates and don't hesitate to let us know via our feedback survey, which products or services you would like to see added next. 

Last update, we announced the new SAP Note Consolidated Release Schedules for SAP BTP and the corresponding initiative of communicating the release schedules of SAP BTP products and services.

We are happy to report that the number of products and services who are communicating their release schedules has further increased to more than 50 now thanks to close collaboration with various SAP BTP teams.

Check the schedules here and from the Release Calendar section of the navigator.

Tip: Did you know you can mark notes as a favorite? If you do so, you will be notified via email whenever the note is being updated. This way you can stay on top of new release schedule communication, like new dates or skipped waves. We recommend you mark all those notes as a favorite that you are interested in receiving updates for.

Fig 11. Where to click to mark a note as a favorite and receive notifications once it gets updated.

Please note: To access SAP Notes you need to log in to SAP for Me using your Universal ID or S-user ID. Read more about the required authorizations here.

Next updates are scheduled on a quarterly basis in alignment with the Harmonized Release Calendar for SAP Cloud Products.*Disclaimer: The schedule is subject to change without prior notice including, when necessary, delays or incorporating changes in subsequent update windows and not a commitment, promise, or legal obligation to deliver any material, code, or functionality.Looking ahead, 2025 updates will mainly focus on quarterly maintenance of release resources and a steady extension of product coverage. Currently, there are no plans for further mayor UI updates in the near future.

The Readiness@Scale team at SAP Enterprise Support is dedicated to ensuring release readiness across the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio. Our mission is to empower SAP customers to be prepared and ready to adopt and utilize innovations.

If you have any feedback or specific requests for content in the release navigator, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

And don't forget to check out our team's blogs tagged with Readiness@Scale.

Happy navigating!

Readiness@Scale 
SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Explore SAP Build Low-Code at TechEd 2024
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is right across the corner and the spotlight shines brighter than ever on SAP Build—a comprehensive suite of low-code and pro-code tools designed to accelerate application development and automation with the power of generative AI. Whether you're an experienced developer or just beginning your digital journey, SAP Build is here to provide business you with the tools needed to simplify app creation, automate processes, and design digital workspaces.

This blog will focus on SAP Build’s low-code offerings at TechEd 2024, highlighting key sessions that showcase how low-code can empower you to drive innovation and transformation within your organization.

SAP TechEd Experience 2024 

SAP TechEd 2024 kicks off with an exciting, free virtual event that’s open to everyone, offering a truly engaging experience no matter where you are. It is a great chance to connect with industry experts, developers, and IT leaders to discover innovative strategies that will elevate your career and drive your company’s success.

   📅 Save the date: October 8–9, 2024 

   🤝 Interactive, accessible, and virtual event

   🚀 Register now! 

Following this, SAP TechEd on Tour will bring deep dive, in-person learning experience to various locations worldwide. 

 

Highlight: SAP Build Low-Code at TechEd virtual 2024 

   🔷 AD100 | Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions

Speakers: Thomas Volmering, Björn Schulz, Alexander Rother 

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 6:00 PM - 6:45 PM CEST

Explore a fresh approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build solutions and the ABAP Cloud development model work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, you can smoothly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs.

   🔷 AD102 | Unlock SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility with SAP Build

Speakers: Florian Buech, Karthik Thirunavukkarasan

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 11:00 AM - 11:25 AM CEST

Explore the latest capabilities in SAP Build solutions to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while keeping your core clean. The extensibility wizard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides direct access to various extension options both on stack and side by side on SAP Business Technology Platform. New remote function call connectivity in SAP Build enables clean core extension of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using tier-two BAPIs.

   🔷 AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build

Speakers: Tim Back, Anne Keller

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.

   🔷 AD108 | Enhance your business efficiency with SAP Build

Speakers: Yumnah Abbasi, Sruthi Chiramel

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:30 PM - 8:55 PM CEST

Find out how customers extend their processes with SAP Build low-code solutions across HR, procurement, supply chain, finance, and sustainability applications. Learn how you can use prebuilt content in SAP Build to accelerate and benefit from the latest features, such as guided experiences.

   🔷 AD109 | Experience the next level of enterprise automation with SAP

Speakers: Seshadri Sreenivas Ramanarayanan, Palak Garg

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 1:00 PM - 1:25 PM CEST

Explore the latest enhancements using SAP Build solutions, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Signavio solutions together. Drive your AI-powered business transformation by combining process analysis, process automation, and integration capabilities. Extend your approval processes with API-based integrations, and find out how you can leverage event-driven architectures, turn process insights and modelling outcomes into actions, and orchestrate your processes at scale in heterogenous landscapes.

   🔷 AD110 | Accelerate development with the latest AI capabilities in SAP Build

Speakers: Rupam Bhattacharya, Marc Huber

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:00 PM - 8:25 PM CEST

Discover the power of Joule in SAP Build solutions to accelerate your development process and AI features in SAP build that improve productivity of its users. Dive into the latest AI features designed for developers, enabling you to extend SAP S/4 HANA. Experience Gen AI powered fusion development firsthand in SAP Build. Learn how to automate processes and work flow productively with AI capabilities. Get a glimpse of what’s new and latest in AI features within SAP Build.

   🔷 AD181 | Develop applications and processes with SAP Build and Joule (Jump-Start session) 

Speakers: Eric Solberg, Patrick Schad

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 9:00 PM - 9:25 PM CEST

Immerse yourself in creating comprehensive intelligent applications using SAP Build solutions in this hands-on session. Gain comprehensive knowledge of full-stack development, process automation, and business site creation with SAP Build. Learn how SAP Cloud Application Programming Model is leveraged in a highly productive development environment with Joule technology.

   🔷 AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills

Speakers: Maria Kondratyeva, Miguel Angel Meza Martinez

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:00 PM - 8:25 PM CEST

Discover the powerful synergy between Joule technology and SAP Build solutions that facilitates a smooth extension-building process. Learn how to enhance AI-powered capabilities in Joule with custom-developed enterprise skills tailored to your company's specific needs. By integrating with both SAP and third-party applications, Joule can instantly apply its newfound knowledge within SAP systems. 

----------------------------------------

In addition to SAP Build highlights, SAP TechEd virtual offers a wide range of sessions, organized into six content tracks. These tracks cover the latest SAP technologies, products, and solutions, ensuring that you can dive deep into the topics most relevant to your role and industry.

   🔍 Browse the full session catalog!

In the Application Development and Automation track, you can dive into the latest innovations in SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Code, SAPUI5, Enterprise Automation with SAP, and more. The track offers practical demos and insights from our top speakers and product experts. 

   👩‍💻 Check out this blogpost for further details!

----------------------------------------

I hope this sneak peek has sparked your excitement to be part of SAP TechEd 2024 and explore all the latest updates around SAP Build and beyond. 

We can’t wait to see you there – Happy TechEd! 😊

entry.title: Enhancing Business Efficiency with SAP Build: Work Zone, Apps, and Process Automation
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) provides a robust suite of tools to help organizations build, extend, and enhance their digital capabilities. Among these tools are SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation. Each tool serves a unique purpose, from creating digital workplaces to developing custom applications and automating processes. This blog introduces these tools, outlines their key features, and provides examples to help you determine when to use each.

Intended Audience

This blog is intended for SAP developers, IT professionals, business process owners, and decision-makers looking to optimize digital experiences, streamline workflows, and accelerate digital transformation. Whether you are a developer seeking low-code solutions, an IT manager focusing on automation, or a business leader aiming to enhance employee productivity, this blog will help you understand how SAP Build tools can drive value for your organization.

Understanding the SAP Build Umbrella

SAP Build is a unified suite within SAP BTP that encompasses SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation. Together, these tools empower both business users and developers to design intuitive user interfaces, create custom applications, and automate complex workflows, all without the need for extensive coding skills.

  • SAP Build Work Zone focuses on creating a personalized digital workspace, where employees can access all their tools and information in one place.
  • SAP Build Apps is a low-code/no-code platform that enables rapid development of custom applications.
  • SAP Build Process Automation allows users to automate repetitive tasks and streamline business processes efficiently.

SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Work Zone enables organizations to create a unified digital workspace that enhances employee experience by integrating multiple applications, data sources, and services into a single, customizable portal. It is ideal for companies aiming to improve employee productivity and engagement by providing a personalized and consolidated workspace.

  • Key Features:
  • Unified access to SAP and third-party apps
  • Customizable user interfaces with drag-and-drop functionality
  • Pre-built templates for quick deployment
  • Role-based content delivery

Example Use Case: Create a central portal for HR that consolidates employee self-service tools, payroll information, training resources, and internal communication platforms.

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps is a low-code/no-code platform that allows users to rapidly develop and deploy custom applications. Designed for both business users and developers, it offers a visual development environment to build responsive applications that can connect to SAP and non-SAP data sources.

  • Key Features:
  • Low-code/no-code application development
  • Drag-and-drop interface for rapid prototyping
  • Pre-built connectors for easy integration with various data sources
  • Flexibility to customize app logic and design

Example Use Case: Develop a mobile application for field service agents to access real-time inventory data, log service requests, and update task statuses without needing extensive coding knowledge.

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Process Automation allows organizations to automate repetitive, manual tasks and streamline business processes using SAP’s AI and machine learning capabilities. This tool is perfect for companies looking to reduce errors, improve efficiency, and lower operational costs.

  • Key Features:
  • Visual workflow design and automation
  • Integration with SAP and third-party systems
  • Pre-built bot templates and AI services
  • Monitoring and analytics for process optimization

Example Use Case: Automate the end-to-end invoice processing workflow, from data extraction to approval, reducing manual intervention and accelerating processing times.

Comparison of SAP Build Tools

 

Conclusion and Next Steps

SAP BTP offers a powerful set of tools — SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation — to help organizations enhance employee experiences, rapidly develop applications, and automate processes. Start by identifying key areas where these tools can provide the most value, and consider initiating a small pilot project to explore their potential benefits. In the next blog, we’ll discuss advanced integration techniques and best practices for maximizing your SAP BTP investments.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps now returns attributes with process instances
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps has flow functions to connect with your process from SAP Build Process Automation. It used to be you had to retrieve a process's instances, and then for each one run a flow function to retrieve that instance’s attributes. Now, the "Retrieve Instances" flow functions returns the attributes with the instances.

Apparently this change was made a while ago, but I just found out by accident. Generally, this makes the "Get Process Attributes" flow function redundant, but it might still be useful to have.

So If I have a process with the following attributes:

If I now run the "Retrieve Instances" flow function.

I will get the attributes along with the instance info.

 

{
    "id": "3696c6d3-99e4-11ef-8976-eeee0a93cf3f",
    "definitionId": "eu10.daniel-wroblewski-free-tier-3dfr434rf.purchaseorderprocessmingforintegration.equipmentPurchaseOrder",
    "definitionVersion": "1",
    "subject": "EquipmentPurchaseOrder",
    "status": "COMPLETED",
    "businessKey": "",
    "parentInstanceId": null,
    "rootInstanceId": "3696c6d3-99e4-11ef-8976-eeee0a93cf3f",
    "applicationScope": "own",
    "projectId": "eu10.daniel-wroblewski-free-tier-34frt56.purchaseorderprocessmingforintegration",
    "projectVersion": "1.0.0",
    "environmentId": "default",
    "attributes": [{
            "id": "comment",
            "label": "Comment",
            "type": "string",
            "value": "Please approve"
        }, {
            "id": "equipmentid",
            "label": "EquipmentID",
            "type": "string",
            "value": "lenovo-2"
        }, {
            "id": "equipmentname",
            "label": "EquipmentName",
            "type": "string",
            "value": "Lenovo Thinkpad X1 - i5 32GB 1TB"
        }, {
            "id": "price",
            "label": "Price",
            "type": "string",
            "value": "11"
        }, {
            "id": "decision",
            "label": "Decision",
            "type": "string",
            "value": null
        }, {
            "id": "managerscomment",
            "label": "Manager's Comment",
            "type": "string",
            "value": null
        }
    ],
    "startedBy": "daniel.wroblewski@sap.com",
    "completedAt": "2024-09-12T08:51:35.592Z",
    "startedAt": "2024-09-12T08:51:34.248Z"
}

 

 

For more info on the integrations between SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Process Automation, see the videos below.

entry.title: SAP at Hackfestival 2024 - Technology & Sustainability Coming Together to Find Innovative Solutions
entry.content:
The Hackfestival 2024 is a highly anticipated tech event that took place in Mannheim between September 6-8. Under the motto “Decarbonize the Planet”, the festival aimed to bring together tech enthusiasts, sustainability experts, coders, and hackers to collaborate and innovate in sustainability related hands-on challenges during a full-weekend hackathon.  

Located at Congress Center Rosengarten, it combined a competitive hackathon and free citizen track with a wide range of engaging activities for the greater public - including an exhibition area, workshops, open discussions and seminars. Participants had the opportunity to network with other tech talents and industry leaders, share their ideas, grow their career, test their skills and learn from each other. 

Apart from that, the event was also a great opportunity to bring citizens closer to sustainability in general as well as individual corporate initiatives. As an event partner, we had many valuable exchanges around SAP’s own journey to net-zero and the SAP Sustainability product portfolio at our booth. It is always meaningful to see the interest in this topic, make a contribution and provide deeper insights into corporate sustainability and thus make it more tangible for a larger audience. 

SAP team at our SAP Sustainability boothSome key facts and figures around the Hackfestival: 

  • Organized by The Hackathon Company – an independent European hackathon-as-a-service provider  
  • 300+ participants 
  • 35+ partners (Google, aws, Roche and more) 
  • 48-hours of hacking 
  • Six sustainability challenges 
  • Price pool of 10.000+ Euros 

The event was kicked-off on Friday with an opening ceremony and inspiring speech by Alexander Gerst, who talked about his journey as an ESA astronaut followed by Christian Specht, mayor of the city of Mannheim. Gunther Rothermel, co-General Manager and Chief Product Officer for SAP Sustainability, and other industry channel partners were part of a fruitful panel discussion, giving insights about the corporate view on sustainability and how technology and innovation can help take climate action and make a difference in the world.  

Gunther said: “With increasing regulatory landscapes and reporting requirements, our customers have set themselves a broad set of challenging sustainability targets...and we did the same at SAP. There is a lot we can address with software and smart approaches to embed sustainability into digital business processes...First we wanted to start by acting as an exemplar, working on our own emissions and journey to net-zero as a company...in the second step, we help our customers to become more sustainable and accompany them with our SAP Sustainability products. Imagine that SAP customers generate 87% of total global commerce....By integrating sustainability more naturally into customers transactions, we can have a huge impact on decarbonization, reduction of waste and more together. So, both these sides matter to us.”  

The panel talk has clearly shown - at SAP, we incorporate sustainability in our strategy as well as our core values and are proud to have a strong internal SAP Community that engages in voluntary projects and lives sustainability in its daily actions, because every contribution, no matter how small, can change things. 

According to this discussion, the focus of our sustainability effort is based on four key areas:  

  • Taking Climate Action and Reducing Carbon Emissions 
  • Establishing a Circular Economy to reuse material and avoid waste 
  • Integrating a Social Responsibility dimension to drive socially responsible value chains, promote respect for human rights, and create positive societal impacts.
  • Holistic Steering & Reporting: Acting on the operational layer to be compliant and safe on the production and plant level as well as in company's own transactions and processes to be transparent on the way to net-zero. 

Sustainability @SAP

Afterwards the different challenges were introduced and kicked off by the companies. The highly motivated participants had 48 hours to solve challenges provided by the “Challenge Setters” under the main topic “Decarbonize the Planet”. Divided into teams they started working on sustainability solutions, including design and business perspectives.  

Coding has started

SAP' challenge: „𝐇𝐞𝐲, 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐞’𝐬 𝐜𝐚𝐫𝐛𝐨𝐧 𝐢𝐧 𝐦𝐲 𝐥𝐮𝐧𝐜𝐡🍴”: 

We provided the following task: “As one-third of global emissions stem from the food system, imagine the impact that can be made by addressing this challenge head-on – especially when there’s the catering needs of over 110,000 employees. ​The challenge for the participants is to develop a digital solution that helps decarbonize the planet by reducing emissions within the food system or specifically targeting food waste.” 

The SAP Challenge

Heads were put together, many innovative ideas were developed and there were no limits to creativity. In the end, we received a lot of valuable ideas, and the following team was among the winners:  

SAP challenge team: "FrostGuardians": Food waste comes from the fact that everyone wastes food every day, so with the help of AI and a camera, the team developed a fridge that manages what's in the fridge, gives tips on food that goes with a recipe and is connected to a shopping list. 

SAP team and the winnersBig congratulations to the team and all other winners for the inspiring and creative solutions. 🏅

"FrostGuardians" the proud winners of the SAP challenge

One of the main goals of the festival was to foster creativity and problem-solving skills among participants and to encourage people to develop solutions with a positive social impact. Many attendees left the festival feeling inspired and motivated to continue their work in the tech industry. 

The Hackfestival 2024 in Mannheim was a fantastic opportunity for tech enthusiasts to come together and push the boundaries of innovation. It provided a platform where like-minded individuals could collaborate and drive positive change. 

It was a pleasure to support this event as a partner. A hackathon is a great opportunity to access collective knowledge, promote creativity and team spirit, resulting in digital applications and innovative ideas that sometimes can even be the start of something big. 

There is only one thing left to say - A big thank you goes to the organizational team and all participants. Only together we can make a difference. 🌍 🌱

Please see the full list of all other winners' solutions (shared by Oliver Bruemmer, Founder & CEO The Hackathon Company):
 
Trash Buddy by Stadt Mannheim/ Metropolregion Rhein-Neckar: Scan products you want to throw away and get tips on what you can do with them. Find other solutions and don’t only throw it away. 
 
Mannheim Green City Quest by Stadt Mannheim/ Metropolregion Rhein-Neckar: Playful knowledge transfer app that encourages young people to try out new ways of thinking and exploring the city in a sustainable way with geolocations and gamification elements. 
 
Rochy - The Carbon Coach by Roche : "Rochy" gives sustainability reminders and awards points when fulfilled and proven with pictures, e.g. plate eaten empty. Collected points lead to coupons or can be donated.  
Carbon Crasher by Schwarz IT KG: Machine learning app that recommends products based on your interests, such as products that save CO2 or have a short expiration date, with special prices, coupons and gamification. 
 
KAEFER by KION Group: Efficient LLM model with key features: dynamic scoring of models, prompt model complexity matching, ML-based prompt categorization, self-learning architecture interaction.

Learn more about SAP Sustainability and subscribe to weekly Sustainability Highlight Newsletter.

entry.title: Performant table list in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Managing and displaying large amounts of data in an application tends to require a lot of consideration, as it is easy to make performance suffer. I recently built a very performant list page for a S/4 HANA Cloud endpoint with 65k+ records using SAP Build Apps which will become available in a template later on, but meanwhile I wanted to share with you my solution so you can already adopt it in your SAP Build Apps application, if you are having performance issues in your list view.

The list page I created has a table type list with sorting, filtering and paging.

Preview of the performance of the table list I created

The main secrets of its great performance are:

  1. When page mounts, it begins to load 50 first items from the database to have something to display while it loads all of the items in the background.
  2. Even when all data is loaded, only 50 items are shown by default, unless the user goes to show the next 50 items, in which case 100 items are shown, and so forth. The way this is done is important – there is a separate data variable that only contains the items shown (BusinessPartnersFiltered). The sorting and filtering is also done into this data variable from the data variable that contains all the data (A_BusinessPartners1).

The core of this logic is placed on the page's logic canvas. The main logic for loading the data happens when the page is mounted, but there are events for when the user wants to see more data (but all data has not loaded yet) and when the data on the page needs sorting/filtering.

Logic of the list page

The page variables and their purposes are the following:

  • dataLoadFail and dataLoadSuccess if the fetching of the full data has failed or succeeded, some logic is run differently, e.g. the "Show more" button will not trigger fetching the next 50 items if all data has already been loaded.
  • showSpinner and showFilterSpinner show spinners in the UI, and block some logic from running by disabling buttons etc.
  • columns list contains the columns, their titles, order and the name of the fields. I use this page variable to avoid needing to change multiple places if I want to change which columns are displayed, or if I would want to change columns dynamically.
  • pageIndex, pageSize, searchQuery, sortBy and sortDirection are used for the implementation of paging, filtering and sorting by column.

As for the logic with which the items from the list containing all the items into the filtered list that is used in the list, I concocted the following formula:

 

 

SLICE(ORDER(IF(!IS_EMPTY(pageVars.searchQuery), SELECT<partner>(data.A_BusinessPartner1, SOME<column>(pageVars.columns, CONTAINS(LOWERCASE(ENCODE_JSON(LOOKUP(partner, column.fieldName))), LOWERCASE(pageVars.searchQuery)))), data.A_BusinessPartner1), IF(!IS_EMPTY(pageVars.sortBy), ENCODE_JSON(LOOKUP(item, pageVars.sortBy)), index), pageVars.sortDirection), 0, pageVars.pageSize)

 

 

This formula first figures out if there is a filter used (= searchQuery is something other than empty), and if yes, it only selects the items from the master list that contain the search query in one of the fields used in the columns. If no, the full master list goes into the next stage. In the next stage it checks if there is a column to sort by (= sortBy is something other than empty), and sorts the filtered master list based on this field into the desired sortDirection. After that the page is cut into size, which is usually 50, unless the user has pressed the Show more button.

There are many additional small details that make me proud of this particular list page, but this concludes the main ones that contribute towards its great performance. If you have any further questions about creating table lists in SAP Build Apps, please comment below.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, September 19th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/19/sap-developer-news-september-19th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Devtoberfest Launch

  • Devtoberfest Community Home: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Events Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Gameboard: https://devrel-tools-prod-scn-badges-srv.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/devtoberfestContest/scnId.Here
  • Devtoberfest Playlist on YouTube: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d&si=R26dQIns-1e97fU0

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/aug24

Devtoberfest Crossword Puzzle

  • https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-sap-crossword.html

Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

  •  https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/transporting-content-in-sap-build-apps-sap-cloud-transport-management/ba-p/13737478

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Devtoberfest Launch
2:09 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release
7:56 Devtoberfest Puzzle
9:06 Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 19th, 2024.

[Sheena] Hello SAP community. The much-awaited Detoberfest 2024 is here, running from September 23rd to October 25th. Devtoberfest helps to prepare for SAP TechEd and offers a mix of educational sessions, hands-on developer tools, and fun activities like Petoberfest, Bugtoberfest, and Spooktoberfest. It is a point-based contest, and the Devtoberfest animated contest game board is already live. You can earn points by attending sessions, completing developer tutorials linked to weekly topics or engaging in activities that benefit the SAP Developer Community.

The grand prize is a retreat at an SAP TechEd Roadshow stop in 2025. Exciting, right? To start participating, join the Devtoberfest group after reviewing the legal terms and conditions posted there.

We will kickstart Devtoberfest on Monday, September 23rd, which is the ABAP/CAP Day. Week 1, we are bringing to you many exciting sessions in ABAP, mainly on clean core extensibility, building partner products on SAP BTP, major updates in embedded analytics, and data integration in ABAP Cloud with SQL services and CDS external entities. Week 1 also covers an exciting session on CAP, it's time to TypeScript (CAP edition).

On tooling Tuesdays, we'll focus on SAP Build Process Automation, Build Code and Build Apps.

Integration Wednesdays will have sessions on FlashPy, CPI Helper, and CPI Lint.

MAD Thursdays will focus on machine learning, AI, and data with sessions on SAP Generative AI Hub, SAP Datasphere, etc.

Frontend Fridays will have sessions on UI5 Freestyle and SAP Fiori tools.

Don't forget to participate in the fun Friday event Petoberfest by posting pictures with your pets at your workstation. So join us in this fun ride of learning and enjoy. Good morning.

[DJ] The latest release of CAP August of 2024, in fact, is out. And with it, there are improvements and fixes to the latest major release 8, which came out earlier this year, in the form of CAP Node.js 8.2.1 and CAP Java 3.2.0. Let me take this opportunity on my walk this morning to go through some of those highlights.

So first up, when the latest major release of CAP came out earlier this year, the new database services went GA, including the database service for SAP HANA. One of the benefits of this new database service for the Node.js runtime is that HANA native associations were no longer required to represent associations at the CDS level. This was great, mostly because it brought longer deploy times when we used SAP HANA Native Associations, plus there was the chance of indirect dependencies also being created. And if you're wondering about the Java runtime, the Java runtime hasn't used native SAP HANA Associations for a while. Anyway, at the time, the release notes advised us to switch off the HANA native associations using configuration. With this August 2024 release, that switch is now automatic. When you start a new project and do, for example, CDS add HANA, you get a switch in the configuration that turns these native associations off by default.

In the area of the CDS language and compiler, you can now use CDS deploy dash dash script to generate a SQL script as a starting point or manual schema migration. Note that in contrast to the existing dash dash dry option, it accepts and produces DDL statements that could lead to data loss. So are A, not supported for automatic schema migration and B, contains relevant warnings in the right places.

In the Node.js runtime, there are also some important changes. One of which is that linked definitions, the class that is used to represent internally structures of services and more, now no longer have the convenience methods such as for each map filter and so on that you would normally expect to have to process the linked definitions as arrays. Because basically linked definitions are not arrays, they're more than that. So these methods have been removed. In my opinion, this is great because it reminds us that we have to treat the data as it is. And so one thing to do is maybe convert the data to arrays using the rest parameter inside, for example, an array, literal array constructor.

Another improvement is that you can now programmatically, i.e. dynamically at runtime, control and activate the CDS debug output at runtime. This means you can turn on debug for cat modules as you go.

In the Java runtime, there's now beta level support for recursive hierarchies with Odata v4 in SAP HANA. Recursive hierarchy is an experimental term in the OData hierarchy vocabulary and is used, for example, with SAP Fiori tree tables. Treetables. You can also now define the name of the Java interfaces generated for CDS entities and structured types using a new annotation. In the multi-tenancy area, activating extensions using the SAP extensibility service can now be done asynchronously. You achieve this by sending a prefer header in the HTTP request with the value of respond async. This in my opinion is a really neat way of coordinating this using the RFC 7240 standard authored by an old acquaintance of mine.

Well I'm almost home but there's just time to tell you about improvements in the tools area. So first for all you IntelliJ users out there the SAP CDS language support plugin is now available on the JetBrains marketplace which means it's directly installable now. Great! For all you TypeScript users there's a new CLI command "CDS TSX", which in contrast to the existing CDS TS command uses a different faster engine. This feature is in beta still at the moment.

So I'm back home. There's one more thing to tell you about and that is if you want to use the SAP Cloud Logging Service instead of the SAP Application Logging Service, you can do that. And there's an option to use telemetry as well, including an option to use Dynatrace. What's not to like? Anyway, that's it for this August 2024 release. See See you next time.

[Shrini and Daniel] Hello, everybody. I'm at Tbilisi, Georgia with Daniel at the EPUB office doing SAP Build CodeJam. It's really great to be here. Yeah, it's been a lot of fun and because it's only a couple days before Devtoberfest, we wanted to tell you something about the contest. So the contest you can earn enough points to get a trip to TechEd perhaps next year and one of the ways to get points is by doing the traditional SAP crossword. So there are topics about SAP Build and JavaScript and even a reference to Analytics Cloud and it would help to know something about Sabrina Carpenter. Can anybody do the Cross Word? Yes, definitely. Of course, it helps to know a little bit about SAP. There are 33 questions. Fairly easy, a few tougher ones, but I think everybody can do it, and it might even be worth a couple thousand points. And it's already published, so even before the Devtoberfest starts, you can accumulate points. So that's pretty good. That's good to know. Yeah. Good luck to everybody.

[Rekha] Hello, everybody. We all know the importance of having development, test, and productive environments during the application development. SAP has recently introduced project versioning for SAP Build apps, web, and mobile projects. This allows you to transport the released project versions between development, testing, and productive subaccounts on SAP BTP. A transport setup of Cloud Transport Management Service, CTMS, Content Agent Service, CAS, and BTP destinations are required to start transporting SAP Build Apps projects between the three tiered dev test prod landscapes. There are four types of subaccounts or tenants involved in the scenario. A source subaccount, this is where the actual development takes place. A target, under this we have two subaccounts, testing, and production, and a central sub-account to administer the content transport between the source and target sub-accounts. In order to enable the transport, you will have to enable the Cloud Foundry, subscribe to SAP Build apps and content agent services, create an instance of content agent, and generate a service key on the source and target sub-accounts.

On the central sub-account, you need to subscribe to Cloud Transport Management service, create an instance of Cloud Transport Management, and generate a service key. The MBS role collection has to be enabled in BTP cockpit to authorize the users for performing the transport activities. SAP Burn Lobby configuration has to be done in the control tower in order to set the sub-account type to decide if it's a dev, test, or prod subaccount. Then BTP destinations has to be set from source to central subaccount, and then from central to target, that is the test and prod subaccounts. Finally, set up the transport landscape using the Cloud Transport Management user interface. For more details on this topic, do refer to this interesting blog post by SAP Build expert Mari Heary. Link is provided in the description box. Enjoy learning and thank you.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling (SAP Build) Sessions
entry.content:
Today is the first day of Devoberfest!!

Make sure you partake of the:

Devtoberfest 2024 sessions

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone
  • SAP Build Code
  • MDK
  • S/4HANA + extensions
  • Integration Suite, including event mesh
  • CAP
  • AI Core
  • GenAI + Joule

That's just one day of Devtoberfest!! In each session, you'll have a chance to ask questions directly to the presenter, who will answer them live or in the chat.

September 24

October 1

October 15

October 22

  • 🟣 Set Up Rules for Who Can Create and Transport SAP Build Projects

  • 🟣 Leveraging Decisions and Business Rules in SAP Build Process Automation
